metadata.title: Why Liquid Helium Refuses to Freeze Even at Absolute Zero
metadata.note: Clean Liam rebuild. Corrects gravitational-binding error and rejects erroneous 3-angstrom/10-meV seed. Scope: helium-4 near saturated vapor pressure; freezes above about 25 bar.

source_of_truth: beat_sheet.json — edit the sheet, not this file

beats.label: This is Liam, in for Bear. Cool helium-four at ordinary pressure toward absolute zero and it still d

beats.prompt: Most substances reach a temperature where binding wins and atoms localize around crystal sites. Helium is the conspicuous exception at low pressure.
beats.label: Most substances reach a temperature where binding wins and atoms localize around crystal sites. Heli

beats.prompt: Absolute zero means the lowest quantum state, not motionless particles with exactly zero kinetic energy.
beats.label: Absolute zero means the lowest quantum state, not motionless particles with exactly zero kinetic ene

beats.prompt: Try to localize one atom tightly around a lattice site. The uncertainty principle forces a broad momentum spread, and therefore nonzero kinetic energy.
beats.label: Try to localize one atom tightly around a lattice site. The uncertainty principle forces a broad mom

beats.prompt: A harmonic estimate captures the floor: every confined mode carries ground-state energy one-half h bar omega.
beats.label: A harmonic estimate captures the floor: every confined mode carries ground-state energy one-half h b

beats.prompt: Helium atoms are exceptionally light. For the same confinement scale, lower mass makes the zero-point wave packet more delocalized and the kinetic cost larger.
beats.label: Helium atoms are exceptionally light. For the same confinement scale, lower mass makes the zero-poin

beats.prompt: Meanwhile two neutral helium atoms attract only through a shallow dispersion interaction. There is no gravitational explanation here; the relevant binding is in
beats.label: Meanwhile two neutral helium atoms attract only through a shallow dispersion interaction. There is n

beats.prompt: At low pressure, the quantum delocalization is large compared with the narrow basin that would hold each atom at a crystal site. The lattice cannot gain enough
beats.label: At low pressure, the quantum delocalization is large compared with the narrow basin that would hold

beats.prompt: So cooling removes thermal motion, but it cannot remove the ground-state spread. Helium remains a quantum liquid all the way toward zero kelvin.
beats.label: So cooling removes thermal motion, but it cannot remove the ground-state spread. Helium remains a qu

beats.label: Pressure changes the balance. Compression brings atoms closer and makes the dense ordered phase favo

beats.label: Helium stays liquid because weak attraction tries to localize atoms while quantum mechanics makes lo
Your turn. If localization length is cut in half, use the uncertainty estimate to decide how the kin
At zero kelvin, thermal motion vanishes. Quantum delocalization does not.
